data.title: Verisk: Robust Q4 Beat, Resilient Outlook, and Transitory Revenue Pressures Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Andrew Nicholas has reiterated their bullish stance on VRSK stock, giving a Buy rating on February 11.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Nicholas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Verisk’s fourth‑quarter performance that materially exceeded both his projections and broader market expectations on revenue, margins, and earnings. The company delivered robust organic growth, particularly in its subscription business, while disciplined cost management translated into stronger‑than‑modeled profitability.

Nicholas also highlights that management’s 2026 guidance, once adjusted for the Verisk Marketing Solutions divestiture, aligns with or slightly exceeds his prior forecasts and appears better than the market had feared, especially after the stock’s notable year‑to‑date pullback. He sees the recent results and outlook as evidence of the business’s resilience through cycles, views current pressures on transactional revenue as transitory, and remains confident that Verisk’s competitive position and pricing power will hold up even as the industry evolves and AI adoption accelerates.

In another report released on February 11, Wells Fargo also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $237.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
